question: When does the special '__init__' method run inside a Python class?

options.A: Only when the program encounters a broken line of code
options.B: Automatically the exact moment a new object is created from the class
options.C: At the very end of the program right before it turns off
options.D: Only if you manually call it by typing '__init__()'

question: Why is the '__init__' method the perfect place to put a 'calibrate()' function for a robot?

options.A: Because it makes sure the robot deletes all its old code first
options.B: Because it forces the calibration to run automatically as part of the setup stage before the robot does anything else
options.C: Because it speeds up the computer screen's colors
options.D: Because it runs inside an infinite loop that never stops

question:
Look at this loop used during calibration:

while not self.bumper.pressing():
    self.BeamArm.spin("forward")

When will the robot arm STOP spinning forward?

options.A: As soon as the bumper sensor is pressed (pressing becomes True)
options.C: As soon as the robot starts driving backward
options.D: It will never stop because it is an infinite loop

options.C: The program crashes with an error
options.D: The computer creates a blueprint but doesn't build the robot

question: What is the purpose of calibration in real-world robotics engineering?

options.A: To translate the code from Python into a different language
options.B: To prepare the hardware by resetting sensors and homing motors so the robot behaves accurately and safely
options.C: To choose a fun name and color for the robot team
options.D: To check if the internet connection is fast enough
